wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Albatal Store project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as a cross-platform e-commerce mobile application built with Flutter, its key features including product catalog, shopping cart, checkout process, and payment integration via Paymob. Document the technology stack including Flutter, Supabase, Riverpod state management, and multi-platform support (Android, iOS, Web, Linux, macOS, Windows). Describe the target audience, business value proposition, and architectural approach using Clean Architecture principles. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical highlights for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and provide practical examples of the app's capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Utilities & Constants
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core utilities and constants module. Document all shared utility functions, helper methods, and constant definitions used throughout the application. Explain the architecture of utility classes, their responsibilities, and usage patterns. Detail error handling utilities, validation helpers, formatting functions, and common business logic abstractions. Include examples of how to use these utilities in feature modules. Document naming conventions, coding standards, and best practices for extending the utility library. Address performance considerations and memory management for utility functions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Architecture Principles
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the core architecture principles of Albatal Store. Explain the Clean Architecture implementation with clear separation between presentation, business logic, and data layers. Document the dependency inversion principle where higher-level modules don't depend on lower-level details. Detail the feature-driven organization pattern where code is organized by business features rather than technical layers. Include architectural decisions, trade-offs, and constraints that shaped the system design. Provide examples of how these principles are applied throughout the codebase with concrete code snippets from main.dart and app.dart.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Registration & Login
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for user registration and login functionality. Detail the implementation of email/password authentication, form validation, error handling, and user session management using Supabase auth. Document the AuthCubit state management patterns, including loading states, success responses, and error scenarios. Explain the database schema for user profiles, authentication policies, and security hardening measures. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing registration flows, login processes, form validation, and error handling. Address password requirements, account verification, and common authentication errors. Document integration with Supabase auth providers and custom authentication logic.

wiki_catalogs.name: Product Listing & Browsing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the product listing and browsing functionality. Document the implementation of product grid/list views, pagination strategies, and infinite scrolling mechanisms. Explain the Cubit state management for product listings, including loading states, error handling, and data synchronization. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing how products are fetched, displayed, and updated in real-time. Document the UI components used for product cards, image loading optimization with caching, and responsive design considerations. Address performance optimizations for large product catalogs, lazy loading strategies, and memory management. Provide guidelines for customizing product display layouts and implementing platform-specific optimizations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Checkout Flow & Address Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the checkout flow and address management system. Explain the complete checkout process from cart review to order confirmation, including address validation, shipping zone calculations, and delivery options. Document the Cubit state management for checkout steps, form validation patterns, and data persistence strategies. Include concrete examples showing address creation, validation rules, and shipping cost calculations. Document the integration with shipping zones database schema and real-time availability updates. Address error handling for invalid addresses, shipping restrictions, and inventory conflicts. Provide troubleshooting guides for common checkout issues and performance optimization tips for large product catalogs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Order Lifecycle Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for order lifecycle management. Explain the complete order flow from creation to completion, including order states (pending, confirmed, processing, shipped, delivered, cancelled). Document order creation workflows, validation rules, and business logic. Include concrete examples of order state transitions, status updates, and lifecycle hooks. Detail the database schema for orders table, order items, and status tracking. Explain order idempotency mechanisms, expiry handling for pending orders, and concurrent order processing safeguards. Address order cancellation processes, refund workflows, and return handling. Include guidelines for implementing custom order states and extending the order lifecycle.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Preferences Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the user preferences management system. Explain how user settings are stored, retrieved, and synchronized across devices. Document the preference data models, validation rules, and default configurations. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing preference updates, persistence strategies using local storage or cloud sync, and preference migration handling. Document the preference API interfaces, event listeners for preference changes, and performance considerations for large preference sets. Address preference security, encryption for sensitive settings, and backup/restore functionality. Include guidelines for adding new preference types and maintaining backward compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Entities & Tables
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Albatal Store's core database entities. Detail the fundamental tables including users, profiles, products, categories, and inventory management. Document field definitions, data types, primary keys, foreign key relationships, and constraints for each core table. Explain the user authentication system integration with Supabase Auth, profile management structure, and product catalog organization. Include entity relationship diagrams showing how core entities connect to each other. Document data validation rules, business constraints, and referential integrity policies. Address data lifecycle management, soft deletes, and archival strategies for core entities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & User Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Supabase authentication and user management in Albatal Store. Document the complete authentication flow including user registration, login, password reset, and session management using Supabase Auth. Explain profile management with custom user data stored in the profiles table. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing authentication state management, protected routes, and user context handling. Document security best practices including email verification, password policies, and session security. Address error handling patterns, loading states, and user experience considerations. Include troubleshooting guides for common authentication issues and debugging techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Unit Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for unit testing implementation in Albatal Store. Document testing patterns for Cubits including state management testing, error handling scenarios, and async operations. Explain repository testing strategies with mocking external dependencies like Supabase services. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing test structure, mock implementations using Mockito or similar libraries, and test data factories. Document testing best practices for business logic validation, edge cases handling, and performance considerations. Address testing challenges for real-time features, payment processing logic, and database interactions. Include guidelines for writing maintainable unit tests, naming conventions, and test organization patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cubit Pattern Fundamentals
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Cubit pattern fundamentals in the Albatal Store application. Explain the core concepts of Riverpod's Cubit implementation, including state classes design, emit patterns, and lifecycle management. Document the standard Cubit structure with concrete examples from auth, catalog, cart, and checkout features. Detail state class conventions, immutable state updates, and proper error handling within Cubits. Include guidelines for creating new Cubits, managing complex state hierarchies, and following project-specific naming conventions. Address common pitfalls and best practices for maintaining predictable state transitions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Android Platform Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Android platform implementation. Document the AndroidManifest.xml configuration including permissions, activities, and service declarations. Explain build.gradle.kts configuration for dependencies, signing configurations, and build variants. Detail the MainActivity.kt implementation and Flutter plugin registration. Cover Gradle wrapper setup, project structure organization, and native code integration patterns. Include concrete examples of permission handling, background services, and Android-specific features. Document debugging techniques using Android Studio, logcat usage, and performance profiling tools. Address APK/AAB building, signing processes, and Google Play Store deployment preparation. Explain platform-specific optimizations, memory management, and battery optimization considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for authentication security implementations in the Albatal Store application. Document secure token management, session handling, and JWT token validation patterns. Explain Supabase authentication integration, user profile security, and password hashing strategies. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing secure login flows, token refresh mechanisms, and session persistence. Document Row Level Security (RLS) policies for authentication data, input validation for user credentials, and protection against common authentication attacks like brute force and credential stuffing. Address secure storage of authentication tokens, automatic logout mechanisms, and multi-device session management. Include guidelines for implementing secure registration flows, email verification, and account recovery processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Image Loading & Caching
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for image loading and caching strategies in Albatal Store. Document the implementation of image caching mechanisms, lazy loading patterns, and memory-efficient image handling. Explain the use of Flutter's ImageProvider, cached_network_image package, and custom image widgets. Include concrete examples showing how product images are loaded, cached, and displayed efficiently. Document image compression strategies, placeholder implementations, and error handling for failed image loads. Address platform-specific image optimization techniques, memory management for large image collections, and performance monitoring for image-heavy screens.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for build configuration across all platforms. Document Flutter project setup in pubspec.yaml including dependencies, assets, and platform-specific configurations. Explain Android build configuration with Gradle scripts, signing configurations, and manifest settings. Cover iOS build configuration including Xcode project setup, signing certificates, and App Store requirements. Document desktop platform builds for Linux, macOS, and Windows including CMake configurations and native dependencies. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing build variants, environment-specific configurations, and optimization flags. Address build performance tuning, caching strategies, and troubleshooting common build issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Button Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for button components in the shared UI library. Document all button variants including primary, secondary, outlined, and icon buttons. Explain props/attributes such as size, color, disabled state, and loading indicators. Include usage examples with code snippets demonstrating different button configurations. Detail styling customization options, theme integration, and accessibility features like keyboard navigation and screen reader support. Document event handling patterns and platform-specific adaptations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Paymob API Endpoints & Webhooks
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Paymob payment gateway endpoints. Document the paymob-initiate endpoint for creating payment sessions, including request parameters, authentication headers, and response schemas. Detail the paymob-callback webhook handler for processing payment notifications, including payload validation, HMAC signature verification, and status updates. Document the checkout endpoint that orchestrates the complete payment flow. Include concrete examples of HTTP requests/responses, error codes, and retry mechanisms. Explain the integration with Supabase edge functions and how they handle asynchronous payment processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Supabase Client Configuration & Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Supabase client configuration and initialization in Albatal Store. Document environment setup, client instantiation patterns, and connection management. Explain configuration options including authentication setup, database connection parameters, and real-time subscription settings. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing proper client initialization, error handling, and connection lifecycle management. Document security considerations including API key management, environment-specific configurations, and connection pooling strategies. Address troubleshooting common connection issues and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Processing Functions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for payment processing edge functions in Albatal Store. Document the Paymob payment gateway integration including payment initiation, callback handling, and HMAC verification. Explain the complete payment flow from checkout to confirmation, including security measures like HMAC signature validation and token management. Include concrete examples showing how paymob-callback processes payment confirmations, validates signatures, and updates order status. Document error handling strategies, retry mechanisms, and idempotency patterns. Address security considerations including secret management, input validation, and secure communication with payment providers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Text Input Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for text input components including TextField, TextFormField, and custom text input widgets. Document input validation patterns, character limits, input types (email, phone, password), and real-time validation feedback. Explain integration with form state management, error display mechanisms, and keyboard handling for mobile platforms. Include examples of secure password inputs, search fields, and formatted text inputs. Detail accessibility features, internationalization support, and performance optimization for large forms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Albatal Store project. Include detailed prerequisites such as Flutter SDK installation, required tools, and environment setup. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including cloning the repository, installing dependencies with flutter pub get, and running the application on different platforms. Document the development workflow including hot reload usage, debugging techniques, and common development tasks. Include platform-specific setup requirements for Android, iOS, Web, and desktop platforms. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Make the content accessible to beginners while ensuring all necessary technical details are covered.

wiki_catalogs.name: Shared UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the shared UI components library. Document all reusable widgets, custom components, and design system implementations. Explain component architecture, props/attributes, events, and customization options. Detail theme integration, styling approaches, and responsive design patterns. Include comprehensive usage examples with code snippets demonstrating component composition and customization. Document accessibility features, internationalization support, and platform-specific adaptations. wiki_catalogs.name: State Management Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Riverpod/Cubit state management strategy used throughout Albatal Store. Explain the Cubit pattern implementation for predictable state updates and reactive programming. Document the dependency injection patterns using Riverpod providers and how they facilitate testability and modularity. Detail the state organization patterns across different features and how shared state is managed. Include practical examples of Cubit implementations, provider definitions, and state transitions. Address error handling patterns, loading states, and performance considerations in state management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Profile Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for user profile management features. wiki_catalogs.name: Search & Filtering
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the search and filtering system. Thoroughly explain the implementation of text-based search, category filtering, price range filters, and advanced search capabilities. Document the database queries and Supabase integration for efficient searching across product attributes. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing search algorithm implementation, filter state management, and real-time search results updates. Explain how to implement custom filters, handle search suggestions, and manage complex query combinations. Address search result ranking, relevance scoring, and handling edge cases like special characters and partial matches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Gateway Integration (Paymob)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for Paymob payment gateway integration. Include concrete examples of payment request schemas, HMAC signature verification, and webhook payload handling. Document the payments table schema, transaction states, and idempotency patterns. Include troubleshooting guides for payment failures, timeout handling, and reconciliation processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Order Status Tracking & Real-time Updates
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for order status tracking and real-time updates. Explain how order statuses are managed, updated, and synchronized across clients. Document the real-time notification system using Supabase subscriptions for live order status updates. Include concrete examples of status change listeners, notification handlers, and UI updates. Detail the notifications table schema, notification types, and delivery mechanisms. Explain push notification integration, in-app notifications, and email notifications for order status changes. Address error handling for failed notifications, retry mechanisms, and notification preferences. Include guidelines for adding new notification types and customizing notification delivery channels.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the internationalization and localization system. wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Database & Data Synchronization

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for payment security implementations and PCI compliance considerations in the Albatal Store application. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing secure checkout flows, payment validation, and error handling. Document idempotency keys to prevent duplicate transactions, payment state management, and secure webhook processing. Address PCI DSS compliance requirements, secure logging practices, and protection against payment fraud. Explain pagination strategies, infinite scrolling implementations, and performance profiling for list-heavy interfaces. Address common performance pitfalls like unnecessary rebuilds, excessive widget creation, and memory leaks in scrollable containers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Protection & Storage Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for data protection and secure storage implementations throughout the Albatal Store application. <blog>

# Albatal Store - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

Albatal Store is a comprehensive Flutter-based e-commerce application designed to provide a complete online shopping experience across multiple platforms (Android, iOS, Web, Linux, macOS, and Windows). The project implements a modern architecture with feature-driven organization, robust state management, and integration with Supabase for backend services and Paymob for payment processing.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Cross-platform compatibility**: Single codebase supporting mobile and desktop platforms
- **Modern architecture**: Clean architecture principles with clear separation of concerns
- **E-commerce functionality**: Complete shopping experience from product browsing to checkout
- **Real-time capabilities**: Live updates and notifications through Supabase
- **Payment integration**: Secure payment processing via Paymob gateway
- **Internationalization**: Multi-language support (Arabic and English)

### Target Audience
- E-commerce businesses seeking a modern, scalable storefront solution
- Developers looking for a reference implementation of Flutter e-commerce applications
- Users requiring a seamless cross-platform shopping experience

## 2. Technical Architecture

The project follows a clean architecture pattern with clear separation between presentation, business logic, and data layers. The architecture emphasizes testability, maintainability, and scalability.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Flutter App] --> B[Presentation Layer]
    B --> C[Business Logic Layer]
    C --> D[Data Layer]
    D --> E[Supabase Backend]
    D --> F[Local Storage]
    
    B --> G[Features]
    G --> H[Auth Feature]
    G --> I[Catalog Feature]
    G --> J[Cart Feature]
    G --> K[Checkout Feature]
    G --> L[Orders Feature]
    
    C --> M[Cubit/State Management]
    C --> N[Services]
    C --> O[Repositories]
    
    D --> P[Supabase Client]
    D --> Q[Local Database]
    D --> R[Cache Layer]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Presentation Layer**: Contains UI components, screens, and widgets organized by features
**Business Logic Layer**: Implements Cubit patterns for state management and business rules
**Data Layer**: Handles data persistence, API calls, and caching strategies
**Feature Modules**: Self-contained modules for each major functionality area

### Design Patterns
- **Clean Architecture**: Clear separation of concerns with dependency inversion
- **Cubit Pattern**: State management using Riverpod's Cubit for predictable state updates
- **Repository Pattern**: Abstraction over data sources for better testability
- **Feature-Driven Organization**: Code organized by business features rather than technical layers

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
The application bootstraps through the main entry point which initializes core dependencies and sets up the app configuration.

**Key Files:**
- `lib/main.dart` - Application entry point and dependency initialization
- `lib/app.dart` - Root widget configuration and theme setup

### Core Modules

**Authentication System:**
- User registration and login functionality
- Profile management and preferences
- Session handling and security

**Product Catalog:**
- Product listing with filtering and search
- Product details and image galleries
- Category navigation and browsing

**Shopping Cart:**
- Add/remove items functionality
- Quantity management
- Price calculations and totals

**Checkout Process:**
- Address management
- Payment integration with Paymob
- Order confirmation and tracking

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Management:**
- Environment-specific configurations for development and production
- Secret management for API keys and sensitive data
- Feature flags and runtime configuration

**Localization:**
- Multi-language support with ARB files
- Dynamic language switching
- Cultural formatting for dates and currencies

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies:**
- Flutter framework for cross-platform development
- Supabase client for real-time database and authentication
- Riverpod for state management
- Dio for HTTP requests and API communication

**Platform-Specific Integrations:**
- Android and iOS native platform channels
- Web-specific optimizations and configurations
- Desktop platform adaptations

### Integration Points

**Supabase Integration:**
- Real-time database synchronization
- Authentication and user management
- File storage for product images
- Edge functions for serverless operations

**Payment Gateway:**
- Paymob integration for secure payment processing
- Payment callback handling
- Transaction status monitoring

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant APP as Flutter App
    participant AUTH as Auth Service
    participant DB as Supabase
    participant PAY as Paymob
    
    U->>APP: Browse Products
    APP->>DB: Fetch Product Data
    DB-->>APP: Return Products
    U->>APP: Add to Cart
    APP->>APP: Update Cart State
    U->>APP: Proceed to Checkout
    APP->>PAY: Initiate Payment
    PAY-->>APP: Payment Response
    APP->>DB: Create Order
    DB-->>APP: Order Confirmation
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**User Management:**
- Secure authentication with email/password
- Profile customization and preferences
- Order history and tracking

**Product Management:**
- Rich product catalog with categories
- Advanced search and filtering
- Product reviews and ratings

**Shopping Experience:**
- Intuitive cart management
- Multiple shipping options
- Real-time inventory updates

**Payment Processing:**
- Secure payment gateway integration
- Multiple payment methods support
- Transaction history and receipts

### Implementation Highlights

**State Management Architecture:**
- Cubit-based state management for predictable updates
- Reactive UI components that respond to state changes
- Error handling and loading states throughout the application

**Data Persistence Strategy:**
- Offline-first approach with local caching
- Real-time synchronization with cloud database
- Optimistic updates for better user experience

**Testing Framework:**
- Comprehensive unit tests for business logic
- Widget tests for UI components
- Integration tests for critical user flows

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Authenticated: Login/Register
    Authenticated --> BrowsingProducts: Browse Catalog
    BrowsingProducts --> InCart: Add to Cart
    InCart --> Checkout: Proceed to Checkout
    Checkout --> PaymentProcessing: Enter Payment Details
    PaymentProcessing --> OrderConfirmed: Payment Success
    PaymentProcessing --> PaymentFailed: Payment Error
    OrderConfirmed --> [*]: Complete
    PaymentFailed --> Checkout: Retry
```

**Performance Optimizations:**
- Image lazy loading and caching
- Efficient list rendering for large catalogs
- Background data synchronization
- Memory management for optimal performance

**Accessibility Features:**
- Screen reader support
- High contrast themes
- Keyboard navigation
- VoiceOver and TalkBack compatibility

---

**Sources:**
- [main.dart](lib/main.dart)
- [app.dart](lib/app.dart)
- [pubspec.yaml](pubspec.yaml)
- [supabase-integration.md](docs/supabase-integration.md)
- [storefront-walkthrough.md](docs/storefront-walkthrough.md)

</blog>
